title: Symmetry Breaking as Quantum Gate: Entropy and Weak Mixing Angle
authors: Qing-Hong Cao, Yandong Liu, Haotian Qi, Hao Zhang, Haoran Zhao
abstract: We establish a correspondence between two independent entropic probes -- the variation of Rényi mutual information (RMI) across the electroweak symmetry breaking (EWSB) transition and the stabilizer Rényi entropy (SRE) -- in tree-level $2\to 2$ elastic scatterings. After angular averaging, the RMI (helicity basis) and the SRE (fixed beam basis) exhibit identical dependence on $\sin^2θ_W$ within each neutral-current channel. We trace this correspondence to a common physical origin that it's the Yukawa mass insertion acts as a $-\mathrm{i}Y$ quantum gate in chirality space. Minimizing entropies across all processes yields $\sin^2θ_W$ values matching purely axial vector-like couplings in $Z$ boson exchanged channel.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the variation in Rényi mutual information and stabilizer Rényi entropy in electroweak scattering follows the same dependence on the weak mixing angle. This shared behavior is explained by treating the Yukawa mass insertion as a quantum gate in chirality space. The results suggest that entropic measures can serve as a diagnostic tool for the chiral structure of particle interactions.
main_problem: The paper investigates the relationship between electroweak symmetry breaking (EWSB) and quantum information theoretic measures, specifically looking for a correspondence between Rényi mutual information and stabilizer Rényi entropy.
main_result: The authors establish that RMI and SRE exhibit an identical dependence on the weak mixing angle after angular averaging, a correspondence traced to the Yukawa mass insertion acting as a -iY quantum gate in chirality space.
method: The study uses helicity amplitude formalism, quantum state tomography to reconstruct density matrices, and angular averaging over the celestial sphere to evaluate entropic probes.
model_or_system: The system consists of 2 to 2 elastic scattering processes (e.g., e- mu- to e- mu-) within the Standard Model framework, specifically focusing on neutral-current channels involving Z boson exchange across the EWSB transition.
key_observables: Rényi mutual information (RMI) in the helicity basis and stabilizer Rényi entropy (SRE) in the fixed beam basis.
important_parameters: Weak mixing angle (sin^2 theta_W), scattering angle (theta), and fermion coupling strengths (kappa_L, kappa_R); high-energy limit (sqrt(s) = 10 TeV).
main_assumptions: The analysis uses the high-energy limit to isolate energy-independent contributions and assumes tree-level 2 to 2 elastic scattering.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a conceptual framework linking QFT and QI; Figure 2 depicts the Yukawa interaction as a quantum gate; Figure 3 illustrates the scattering process before and after EWSB; Figures 4 and 5 show the dependence of entropic measures on scattering angles and the weak mixing angle.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a unified framework for QFT and QI, describes the scattering model and the quantum gate interpretation of mass insertion, presents the calculation of entropic probes, analyzes the correspondence between RMI and SRE, and discusses the diagnostic role of these measures.
why_interesting: It provides a novel bridge between high-energy particle physics and quantum information theory by interpreting fundamental symmetry breaking as a specific quantum gate operation acting on chirality space.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

As discussed in [9, 10], this distinction originates from the global Lorentz rotation relating the helicity and the fixed beam computational bases.
